ops.title: ccx-channel trial: exposing ccx to codex does not beat the plain baseline
ops.body:
18-run A/B trial (2026-07-15) on whether to expose ccx to `codex-ask` invocations. 3 heavy-context tasks (large-diff review with 5 planted defects; cross-file subcommand enumeration; scoped verification) × 3 channels × 2 reps, judged by opus against fixed ground truth. Channels: `cli` = ccx cheat-sheet appended to developer_instructions; `mcp` = cc-context MCP server wired via `-c mcp_servers.cc-context`; `off` = neither (baseline). All runs `--sandbox danger-full-access`, so ccx is on PATH in every channel.

Result — no clean win for exposure; the baseline wins:

| channel | correct/reps | avg tool calls | avg wall s | avg input tok | MCP calls |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| cli | 5/6 | 20.7 | 195 | 666k | 0 |
| mcp | 5/6 | 20.7 | 181 | 750k | 0 |
| off | 5/6 | 13.0 | 161 | 467k | 0 |

- **Correctness identical (5/6 each).** All three misses are codex run-variance, not channel effects: cli and mcp each hallucinated a phantom stderr warning on Task C; off punted once on Task B. Task A was a perfect 5/5 for all six runs.
- **`off` is leanest on every metric** — fewest tool calls, fastest, fewest tokens.
- **MCP server never used: 0 MCP tool calls across all 18 runs** (and all 4 pilots). codex ignores the wired cc-context server and shells out to `ccx` (or plain reads) instead, because ccx is already on PATH. The `mcp` channel is pure overhead (its server offering inflates input tokens).
- **The `cli` cheat-sheet is net-negative** — it induces more gathering (20.7 vs 13.0 calls) with no correctness gain.

**How to apply:** don't wire ccx into codex-ask. codex already discovers and uses ccx via the shell under `danger-full-access`; explicit exposure (cheat-sheet or MCP) adds no correctness and costs efficiency. Keep codex-ask lean. This refutes the intuitive "expose ccx to help with heavy-token context" hypothesis — the ambient PATH availability is sufficient. See [[codex-security-review-cyber-classifier]] for the sibling codex-lane finding.

Trial harness + raw logs: /tmp/ccx-trial (benchmark, ground truth, parse-metrics.py, per-run logs).
